questions.question: Under s.509 CA 2014, what is the fundamental eligibility criterion for a company to be placed under the protection of the court via examinership?

options.A: The company must be already insolvent and unable to pay debts as they fall due
options.B: The company must be, or likely to be, unable to pay its debts, AND the court must be satisfied that there is a reasonable prospect of the company's survival as a going concern
options.C: The company must have a minimum of 50 employees to qualify for examinership
options.D: The company must have been trading for at least two years before petitioning for examinership

questions.explanation: s.509 CA 2014 requires: (1) the company is or is likely to be unable to pay its debts; AND (2) there is a reasonable prospect of survival of the company and the whole or any part of its undertaking as a going concern. Both conditions must be satisfied — a company that is already terminally insolvent without rescue prospects cannot be placed in examinership.

questions.question: Under s.520 CA 2014, what is the maximum duration of the examinership protection period, and under what circumstances may it be extended?

options.A: 50 days; may be extended to 70 days by court order where the examiner demonstrates significant progress
options.B: 70 days; may be extended to 100 days by court order where the examiner demonstrates significant progress in formulating the scheme of arrangement
options.C: 90 days; no extension is possible — the examiner must report within 90 days or the protection lapses
options.D: 60 days; may be extended to 120 days at the court's discretion without conditions

questions.explanation: Under s.520 CA 2014, the initial protection period is 70 days. The court may extend this by up to 30 additional days (to a maximum of 100 days) where the examiner applies and demonstrates that the examiner is likely to be able to formulate proposals for a scheme of arrangement. The total maximum period is therefore 100 days.

questions.question: In Re Vantive Holdings Ltd [2010] IEHC, the High Court considered the threshold for appointing an examiner. What key principle regarding the 'reasonable prospect' of survival did the court articulate?

options.A: The court must be satisfied that survival of the company is certain before appointing an examiner — a mere possibility is insufficient
options.B: The threshold for 'reasonable prospect' is low — the petitioner need only show that survival is not a remote possibility. The court should not engage in a detailed merits analysis at the petition stage
options.C: The court requires independent expert evidence from an investment bank confirming that the company has a greater than 50% probability of survival
options.D: A 'reasonable prospect' requires the petitioner to have secured committed third-party investment before the petition is heard

questions.explanation: In Re Vantive Holdings [2010] IEHC, the High Court held that the 'reasonable prospect' threshold does not require certainty or even probability — it is sufficient that survival is a realistic possibility and not too remote. The court should not conduct an in-depth merits analysis at the threshold stage; a prima facie case of survival prospect is sufficient for appointment.

questions.question: Under s.520A CA 2014 (ipso facto clause prohibition, as amended), what protection does examinership provide in relation to contracts containing ipso facto clauses?

options.A: Ipso facto clauses in contracts are automatically void once a company enters examinership — all contracts continue on their existing terms
options.B: Counterparties to contracts may not exercise termination rights that arise solely because of the commencement of examinership proceedings — ipso facto clauses triggered purely by the insolvency appointment are unenforceable during the protection period
options.C: Ipso facto clauses remain fully enforceable — counterparties may terminate contracts for any reason during the examinership protection period
options.D: s.520A applies only to employment contracts and lease agreements — commercial supply contracts retain full ipso facto clause enforceability

questions.explanation: s.520A CA 2014 prevents counterparties from relying solely on the commencement of examinership as a trigger to terminate, suspend, or alter contracts under ipso facto clauses. This protects the company's ability to continue trading and preserves the value of its business during the protection period. Counterparties may still terminate for independent contractual breaches.

questions.question: In Re Ina's Kitchen Ltd [2022] IEHC, the High Court examined the obligations of an examiner and the viability of a proposed scheme. What principle regarding the examiner's independence was reinforced?

options.A: The examiner may act as an agent of the majority creditors who support the rescue plan
options.B: The examiner must act as an independent officer of the court, not as an agent of either the company or any particular creditor class — the examiner's duty is to the court and the process
options.C: The examiner owes fiduciary duties exclusively to the company's shareholders as the ultimate risk-bearers
options.D: The examiner may accept a retainer from the company's largest secured creditor provided this is disclosed to the court

questions.explanation: Re Ina's Kitchen [2022] IEHC reinforced that the examiner is an officer of the court whose overriding duty is to the court and the examinership process, not to any stakeholder group. An examiner must maintain independence throughout the process, and any conflict of interest must be disclosed to and adjudicated upon by the court.

questions.question: SI 380/2022 (European Communities (Companies) (Cross-Class Cram-Down) Regulations 2022) introduced a 'cross-class cram-down' mechanism into Irish examinership. Under s.521A CA 2014, what is required for the court to impose a scheme on a dissenting class of creditors?

options.A: A majority of all creditor classes must approve the scheme; dissenting creditors may appeal within 21 days
options.B: At least one 'in the money' class of affected creditors must approve the scheme, and the court must be satisfied that: the dissenting class receives at least as much as they would receive in liquidation; no class receives more than the full value of its claims; and the best interests of creditors test is met
options.C: The court may impose a cram-down only where the dissenting class is a preferential creditor class
options.D: Cross-class cram-down requires the unanimous approval of all secured creditors before the court can override objections from unsecured creditor classes

questions.explanation: Under s.521A CA 2014 (implementing the EU Restructuring Directive via SI 380/2022), the cross-class cram-down allows a court to confirm a scheme over a dissenting class provided: (1) at least one in-the-money class approves; (2) the dissenting class receives at least the liquidation value of their claim (no creditor worse off test); (3) no class receives more than 100% of its claim; and (4) the scheme is fair and equitable. This is a significant extension of the examiner's powers.

questions.question: Re New Look Retailers (Ireland) Ltd [2021] IEHC considered the treatment of landlord claims in an examinership scheme. What challenge did this case highlight regarding the use of examinership against secured or semi-secured creditor classes?

options.A: The court held that all landlord claims are fully secured and cannot be impaired in any examinership scheme
options.B: The case highlighted the tension between the examiner's need to restructure high-cost leases and the landlords' status as creditors with substantial claims — the court examined whether impairment of landlord claims in a scheme was consistent with the 'no creditor worse off' principle when compared to liquidation outcomes
options.C: The court held that leases must be assigned to a new entity before examinership can be used to impair rental obligations
options.D: The court held that the examiner has no power to impair lease obligations — only financial creditors' claims may be restructured in examinership

questions.explanation: Re New Look Retailers (Ireland) [2021] IEHC dealt with the treatment of landlords' lease obligations in the examinership scheme. The court examined whether the scheme satisfied the 'no creditor worse off' test compared to a hypothetical liquidation — if landlords would receive less under the scheme than in liquidation, the scheme cannot be imposed. The case was significant for its analysis of how lease restructuring interacts with the court's confirmation jurisdiction.

questions.question: During the examinership protection period, can a secured creditor enforce their security (e.g. appoint a receiver) against the company's assets?

options.A: Yes — secured creditors' enforcement rights are unaffected by court protection in examinership
options.B: No — under s.520 CA 2014, once the company is under court protection, no receiver may be appointed and no proceedings (including enforcement of security) may be commenced or continued against the company without court consent
options.C: Secured creditors may enforce their security during examinership but only up to the value of the secured asset
options.D: Enforcement of security is automatically stayed for 30 days from the appointment of the examiner, after which secured creditors may proceed

questions.explanation: Under s.520(1) CA 2014, during the period of court protection, no receiver may be appointed over company property and no proceedings may be instituted or continued against the company without leave of the court. This moratorium applies to secured creditors as well, protecting the company's assets from piecemeal enforcement during the restructuring process.

questions.question: Who may petition for the appointment of an examiner under CA 2014?

options.A: Only the company itself or its directors may petition — creditors have no standing to petition for examinership
options.B: The company, its directors, a creditor (including contingent/prospective creditors), or a member holding at least 10% of paid-up voting capital
options.C: Only the company acting by special resolution of shareholders
options.D: Any interested party, including employees, trade unions, and regulatory bodies

questions.explanation: Under s.509(2) CA 2014, an application for the appointment of an examiner may be made by the company, the directors of the company, a creditor (including a contingent or prospective creditor), or a member holding not less than 10% of the paid-up voting capital. The petitioner must also present an independent expert's report under s.511.

questions.question: What is the content requirement for the Independent Expert's Report that must accompany a petition for examinership under s.511 CA 2014?

options.A: A report from the company's own auditors confirming that the company has been solvent for at least 18 months
options.B: A report from an independent accountant (a qualified person) assessing whether the company has a reasonable prospect of survival as a going concern, with reasons, and stating the likely outcome for creditors in an examinership versus liquidation
options.C: A report from a licensed insolvency practitioner confirming that no alternative to examinership exists
options.D: A report from the company's bankers confirming that further credit facilities are available to fund the examinership

questions.explanation: Under s.511 CA 2014, the petition must be accompanied by a report from an independent expert (a qualified accountant) that: (a) states whether the company has a reasonable prospect of survival; (b) states the facts and assumptions on which that opinion is based; and (c) provides an estimate of the company's assets and liabilities and the likely outcome for creditors in a winding up. This report is central to the court's assessment of the petition.
